title: Auto injector with detection of used cartridge and associated method
abstract: Disclosed is an auto-injector for administering a medicament, comprising: a housing; a cartridge receiver configured to receive a cartridge comprising a first stopper; a drive module coupled to move a plunger rod configured to move the first stopper; a resistance sensor configured to provide a resistance signal indicative of resistance against movement of the plunger rod; and a processing unit coupled to the drive module and to the resistance sensor. The processing unit being configured to: control the drive module to move the plunger rod towards the extended plunger rod position; determine present plunger rod position; receive the resistance signal; determine a cartridge parameter based on the resistance signal and the present plunger rod position; and control the drive module to adjust the movement of the plunger rod based on the cartridge parameter.
claims:
Claims 1 . An auto-injector for administering a medicament, comprising: a housing; a cartridge receiver configured to receive a cartridge comprising a first stopper; a drive module coupled to move a plunger rod between a retracted plunger rod position and an extended plunger rod position, the plunger rod being configured to move the first stopper; a resistance sensor configured to provide a resistance signal indicative of resistance against movement of the plunger rod; and a processing unit coupled to the drive module and to the resistance sensor; the processing unit being configured to: - control the drive module to move the plunger rod towards the extended plunger rod position; - determine present plunger rod position; - receive the resistance signal; - determine a cartridge parameter based on the resistance signal and the present plunger rod position; and - control the drive module to adjust the movement of the plunger rod based on the cartridge parameter, wherein the processing unit is configured to control the drive module to adjust movement of the plunger rod if the cartridge parameter is indicative of the present plunger rod position having reached a plunger rod threshold and the resistance signal being indicative of resistance against movement of the plunger rod below a low resistance threshold. 2. Auto injector according to claim 1 , wherein the processing unit is further configured to control the drive module to adjust movement of the plunger rod if the cartridge parameter is indicative of the resistance signal 

 being indicative of resistance against movement of the plunger rod above a high resistance threshold. 3. An auto-injector for administering a medicament, comprising: a housing; a cartridge receiver configured to receive a cartridge comprising a first stopper; a drive module coupled to move a plunger rod between a retracted plunger rod position and an extended plunger rod position, the plunger rod being configured to move the first stopper; a resistance sensor configured to provide a resistance signal indicative of resistance against movement of the plunger rod; and a processing unit coupled to the drive module and to the resistance sensor; the processing unit being configured to: - control the drive module to move the plunger rod towards the extended plunger rod position; - determine present plunger rod position; - receive the resistance signal; - determine a cartridge parameter based on the resistance signal and the present plunger rod position; and - control the drive module to adjust the movement of the plunger rod based on the cartridge parameter, wherein the processing unit is configured to control the drive module to adjust movement of the plunger rod if the cartridge parameter is indicative of the resistance signal being indicative of resistance against movement of the plunger rod above a high resistance threshold. 4. Auto injector according to claim 3, wherein the processing unit is further configured to control the drive module to adjust movement of the plunger rod if the cartridge parameter is indicative of the present plunger rod 

 position having reached a plunger rod threshold and the resistance signal being indicative of resistance against movement of the plunger rod below a low resistance threshold. 5. An auto-injector for administering a medicament, comprising: a housing; a cartridge receiver configured to receive a cartridge comprising a first stopper; a drive module coupled to move a plunger rod between a retracted plunger rod position and an extended plunger rod position, the plunger rod being configured to move the first stopper; a resistance sensor configured to provide a resistance signal indicative of resistance against movement of the plunger rod, the resistance sensor being configured to determine electrical power consumed by the drive module; and a processing unit coupled to the drive module and to the resistance sensor; the processing unit being configured to: - control the drive module to move the plunger rod towards the extended plunger rod position; - determine present plunger rod position; - receive the resistance signal; - determine a cartridge parameter based on the resistance signal and the present plunger rod position; and - control the drive module to adjust the movement of the plunger rod based on the cartridge parameter. 6. Auto injector according to claim 5, wherein the processing unit is configured to control the drive module to adjust movement of the plunger rod if the cartridge parameter is indicative of the present plunger rod position having reached a plunger rod threshold and the resistance signal being 

 indicative of resistance against movement of the plunger rod below a low resistance threshold. 7. Auto injector according to any of claims 5-6, wherein the processing unit is configured to control the drive module to adjust movement of the plunger rod if the cartridge parameter is indicative of the resistance signal being indicative of resistance against movement of the plunger rod above a high resistance threshold. 8. Auto injector according to any of the preceding claims, wherein adjusting the movement of the plunger rod comprises stopping the movement of the plunger rod. 9. Auto injector according to any of the preceding claims, wherein adjusting the movement of the plunger rod comprises moving the plunger rod to the retracted plunger rod position. 10. Auto injector according to any of the preceding claims comprising a code sensor configured to read a cartridge code feature, the processing unit being coupled to the code sensor, the processing unit being configured to receive from the code sensor a code signal indicative of the cartridge code feature and determine the plunger rod threshold and/or the low resistance threshold and/or the high resistance threshold based on the code signal. 1 1 . Auto injector according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the resistance sensor is configured to determine electrical power consumed by the drive module. 12. Auto injector according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the resistance sensor is configured to measure pressure and/or force applied to a plunger rod front end of the plunger rod. 13. Auto injector according to any of the preceding claims comprising a tachometer configured to provide a tachometer signal indicative of a count of revolutions of the drive module, the processing unit being coupled to the tachometer, and the processing unit being configured to receive the tachometer signal and determine the present plunger rod position based on the tachometer signal. 14. Auto injector according to any of the preceding claims comprising a user interface coupled to the processing unit, the processing unit being configured to output a user output signal via the user interface based on the cartridge parameter. 15. A system comprising an auto injector according to any of the preceding claims, and a cartridge comprising a first stopper, wherein the cartridge is configured to be received in the cartridge receiver. 16. Method for controlling an auto injector, the method comprising: - receiving a cartridge comprising a first stopper; - moving a plunger rod towards an extended plunger rod position; - determining present plunger rod position; - receiving a resistance signal indicative of resistance against movement of the plunger rod; - determining a cartridge parameter based on the resistance signal and the present plunger rod position; and - adjusting the movement of the plunger rod based on the cartridge parameter if the cartridge parameter is indicative of the present plunger rod position having reached a plunger rod threshold and the resistance signal being indicative of resistance against movement of the plunger rod below a low resistance threshold. 17. Method according to claim 16 further comprising adjusting the movement of the plunger rod based on the cartridge parameter if the cartridge parameter is indicative of the resistance signal being indicative of resistance against movement of the plunger rod above a high resistance threshold. 18. Method for controlling an auto injector, the method comprising: - receiving a cartridge comprising a first stopper; - moving a plunger rod towards an extended plunger rod position; - determining present plunger rod position; - receiving a resistance signal indicative of resistance against movement of the plunger rod; - determining a cartridge parameter based on the resistance signal and the present plunger rod position; and - adjusting the movement of the plunger rod based on the cartridge parameter if the cartridge parameter is indicative of the resistance signal being indicative of resistance against movement of the plunger rod above a high resistance threshold. 19. Method according to claim 18 further comprising adjusting the movement of the plunger rod based on the cartridge parameter if the cartridge parameter is indicative of the present plunger rod position having reached a plunger rod threshold and the resistance signal being indicative of resistance against movement of the plunger rod below a low resistance threshold. 20. Method for controlling an auto injector, the method comprising: - receiving a cartridge comprising a first stopper; - moving a plunger rod towards an extended plunger rod position; - determining present plunger rod position; 

 - receiving a resistance signal indicative of resistance against movement of the plunger rod, the resistance signal being based on electrical power consumed by the drive module; - determining a cartridge parameter based on the resistance signal and the present plunger rod position; and - adjusting the movement of the plunger rod based on the cartridge parameter. 21 . Method according to claim 20 further comprising adjusting the movement of the plunger rod based on the cartridge parameter if the cartridge parameter is indicative of the resistance signal being indicative of resistance against movement of the plunger rod above a high resistance threshold. 22. Method according to any of claims 20-21 further comprising adjusting the movement of the plunger rod based on the cartridge parameter if the cartridge parameter is indicative of the present plunger rod position having reached a plunger rod threshold and the resistance signal being indicative of resistance against movement of the plunger rod below a low resistance threshold. 23. Method according to any of claims 16-22, wherein adjusting the movement of the plunger rod comprises stopping the movement of the plunger rod. 24. Method according to any of claims 16-23, wherein adjusting the movement of the plunger rod comprises moving the plunger rod to a retracted plunger rod position. 25. Method according to any of claims 16-24 comprising receiving a code signal indicative of a cartridge code feature and determining the plunger rod 

 threshold and/or the low resistance threshold and/or the high resistance threshold based on the code signal.
